title: General Wind Load Design Requirements
description: Requirement that all buildings and structures resist wind loads per Chapters 26-31

data.rule: Buildings and other structures, including the main wind force resisting system (MWFRS) and all components and cladding (C&C) thereof, shall be designed and constructed to resist the wind loads determined in accordance with Chapters 26 through 31
data.then: Design per Chapters 26-31

title: Tornado Load Requirements for Risk Category III and IV
description: Additional tornado load requirements for higher risk category buildings

data.rule: Risk Category III and IV buildings and other structures, including the MWFRS and all C&C thereof, shall also be designed and constructed to resist tornado loads determined in accordance with Chapter 32, as applicable
data.then: Design for tornado loads per Chapter 32

title: Main Wind Force Resisting System Procedures
description: Permitted procedures for determining MWFRS wind loads

data.rule: Wind loads for the MWFRS shall be determined using one of the following procedures
data.then: Use one of: 1) Directional Procedure for buildings of all heights (Chapter 27), 2) Envelope Procedure for low-rise buildings (Chapter 28), 3) Directional Procedure for building appurtenances and other structures (Chapter 29), or 4) Wind Tunnel Procedure (Chapter 31)

title: Components and Cladding Procedures
description: Permitted procedures for determining C&C wind loads

data.rule: Wind loads on C&C on all buildings and other structures shall be designed using one of the following procedures
data.then: Use either: 1) Analytical Procedures provided in Parts 1 through 5, as appropriate, of Chapter 30; or 2) Wind Tunnel Procedure as specified in Chapter 31

title: Performance-Based Procedures
description: Requirements for using performance-based wind design procedures

data.rule: Wind design of buildings and other structures using performance-based procedures shall be permitted subject to the approval of the Authority Having Jurisdiction
data.then: The performance-based wind design procedures used shall, at a minimum, conform to Section 1.3.1.3

data.definition: Acceptable to the Authority Having Jurisdiction
The ASCE database (version 2022-1.0) of geocoded wind speed design data

title: ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase
description: Reference to external ASCE wind speed database

data.definition: A horizontal (maximum slope of 2%) patio cover attached to the building wall at any height; it is different from an overhang, which is an extension of the roof surface
Three-second gust speed at 33 ft (10 m) above the ground in Exposure C (see Section 26.7.3) as determined in accordance with Section 26.5.1
A building supported on structural elements where wind can pass beneath the building
A building that has the total area of openings in each wall that receives positive external pressure less than or equal to 4 ft² (0.37 m²) or 1% of the area of that wall, whichever is smaller

title: Enclosed Building Opening Requirement
description: Formula for determining if a building meets the enclosed building criteria

data.expression: A_o < 0.01A_g or 4 ft² (0.37 m²); whichever is smaller

A_o.source: total area of openings in the wall that receives positive external pressure

A_g.source: gross surface area of that wall in which A_o is identified

data.definition: An enclosed, partially enclosed, or partially open building that complies with the following conditions

title: Figure 26.1-1: Outline of process for determining wind loads
description: Flowchart showing the basic parameters and procedures for determining wind loads on MWFRS and C&C

data.description: Flowchart outlining the process for determining wind loads, linking Chapter 26 basic parameters to MWFRS procedures in Chapters 27, 28, 29, and 31 and C&C procedures in Chapters 30 and 31.

data.definition: A building that has each wall at least 80% open. This condition is expressed for each wall by the equation A_o ≥ 0.8A_g where A_o is the total area of openings in the wall that receives positive external pressure, ft² (m²), and A_g is a gross surface area of that wall in which A_o is identified, ft² (m²).

title: Open Building Wall Opening Requirement
description: Formula for determining if a wall meets the open building criteria

A_o.source: total area of openings in the wall that receives positive external pressure

A_g.source: gross surface area of that wall in which A_o is identified

data.definition: A building that complies with both of the following conditions: 1. Mean roof height h less than or equal to 60 ft (18 m), and 2. Mean roof height h does not exceed the least horizontal dimension. These conditions are expressed by the equations A_o > 4 ft² (0.37 m²) or > 0.01A_g; whichever is smaller; and A_oi/A_gi ≤ 0.20
A building that does not comply with the requirements for open, partially enclosed, or enclosed buildings.

title: Building, Torsionally Regular Under Wind Load

data.term: BUILDING, TORSIONALLY REGULAR UNDER WIND LOAD
data.definition: A building with the MWFRS about each principal axis proportioned so that the maximum displacement at each story under Case 2, the torsional wind load case, of Figure 27.3-8 does not exceed the maximum displacement at the same location under Case 1 of Figure 27.3-8, the basic wind load case.
Cladding, roofing, exterior walls, glazing, door assemblies, window assemblies, skylight assemblies, and other components enclosing the building.
Slender buildings and other structures that have a fundamental natural frequency less than 1 Hz.

title: Partially Enclosed Building Opening Area Comparison
description: Formula for determining if opening area in one wall exceeds balance of building envelope

A_o.source: total area of openings in a wall that receives positive external pressure

A_oi.source: sum of the areas of openings in the building envelope (walls and roof), not including A_o

data.definition: A procedure for determining wind loads on buildings and other structures for specific wind directions, in which the external pressure coefficients used are based on past wind tunnel testing of prototypical building models for the corresponding direction of wind.

Areas vulnerable to hurricanes; in the United States and its territories, these are defined as 1. The US Atlantic Ocean and Gulf of Mexico coasts where the basic wind speed for Risk Category II buildings is greater than 115 mi/h (51.4 m/s); and 2. Hawaii, Puerto Rico, Guam, US Virgin Islands, Northern Mariana Islands, and American Samoa.
With respect to topographic effects in Section 26.8, a land surface characterized by strong relief in any horizontal direction.
Construction that has been shown by testing to withstand the impact of test missiles and that is applied, attached, or locked over exterior glazing.
An assemblage of structural elements assigned to provide support and stability for the overall building or other structure. The system generally receives wind loading from more than one surface.

The average of the roof eave height and the height to the highest point on the roof surface, except that, for roof angles less than or equal to 10 degrees, the mean roof height is permitted to be taken as the roof eave height.
Effective wind area, ft² (m²)

title: Sign Convention
description: Establishes the sign convention for wind pressure direction

data.rule: Positive pressure acts toward the surface, and negative pressure acts away from the surface
data.then: Use positive sign for pressure acting toward surface, negative sign for pressure acting away from surface

title: Critical Load Condition
description: Requirement for combining external and internal pressures

data.rule: Values of external and internal pressures shall be combined algebraically to determine the most critical load
data.then: Combine external and internal pressures algebraically to find most critical load condition

title: Wind Pressures Acting on Opposite Faces of Each Building Surface
description: Requirement to account for pressures on both faces of building surfaces

data.rule: In the calculation of design wind loads for the MWFRS and for C&C for buildings, the algebraic sum of the pressures acting on opposite faces of each building surface shall be taken into account
data.then: Calculate algebraic sum of pressures on opposite faces of each building surface

title: Basic Wind Speed Determination
description: Requirements for determining basic wind speed by risk category

data.rule: The basic wind speed, V, used in the determination of design wind loads on buildings and other structures shall be determined from Figure 26.5-1 based on Risk Category
data.then: Use Figure 26.5-1A for Category I, Figure 26.5-1B for Category II, Figure 26.5-1C for Category III, Figure 26.5-1D for Category IV

title: Alternative Basic Wind Speed Source
description: Permission to use ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase as alternative to wind maps

data.rule: It shall be permitted to use the basic wind speeds from the ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase for the contiguous United States, Alaska, and selected special wind regions
data.then: May use ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase instead of Figure 26.5-1

title: ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase
description: External database for basic wind speeds

parameters: basic wind speeds for contiguous United States
basic wind speeds for selected special wind regions

title: ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase for Location-Specific Wind Speeds
description: Reference to external database for determining location-specific basic wind speeds

title: Linear Interpolation Between Wind Speed Contours
description: Permission to use linear interpolation between contours with point values to aid interpolation

data.rule: Linear interpolation is permitted between contours
data.then: Point values are provided to aid with interpolation

title: Wind Speed Assignment for Areas Outside Contours
description: Requirement for islands, coastal areas, and land boundaries outside the last contour

data.rule: Islands, coastal areas, and land boundaries outside the last contour shall use the last wind speed contour
data.then: Use the last wind speed contour value

title: Location-Specific Basic Wind Speeds Using Database
description: Permission to determine location-specific basic wind speeds using ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase

data.rule: Location-specific basic wind speeds shall be permitted to be determined using the ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase
data.then: Use ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase for location-specific values

title: Basic Wind Speeds for US Territories
description: Wind speed values for US territories and special regions

title: Figure 26.5-1B: Basic wind speeds for Risk Category II buildings and other structures
description: Wind speed map for Risk Category II buildings and other structures

data.description: Basic wind speed contour map for Risk Category II buildings and other structures.

title: Basic Wind Speeds for U.S. Territories
description: Wind speeds for American Samoa, Guam, Northern Mariana Islands, Hawaii, Puerto Rico, and U.S. Virgin Islands

title: Wind Speed Determination for Specific Territories
description: Requirement to use ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase for certain territories

data.rule: Wind speeds for Hawaii, US Virgin Islands, and Puerto Rico shall be determined from the ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase
data.then: Use ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase

title: Special Wind Region Examination
description: Requirements for examining special terrain features for unusual wind conditions

data.rule: Mountainous terrain, gorges, ocean promontories, and special wind regions shall be examined for unusual wind conditions
data.then: Examine for unusual wind conditions. Site-specific values for selected special wind regions shall be permitted to be determined using the ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase

title: Wind Speed Probability and Return Period
description: Statistical basis for wind speeds in Risk Category IV

data.rule: Wind speeds correspond to approximately a 15% probability of exceedance in 50 years
data.then: Annual Exceedance Probability = 0.00033, MRI = 3,000 years

title: Special Wind Regions Examination
description: Requirements for examining special wind regions for unusual conditions

data.rule: Special wind regions shall be examined for unusual wind conditions and Authority Having Jurisdiction shall adjust values if necessary

conditions.value: mountainous terrain, gorges, and special wind regions

data.then: Authority Having Jurisdiction shall adjust values in Figure 26.5-1 based on meteorological information and basic wind speed estimation per Section 26.5.3

title: ASCE 7 Wind Design Geodatabase for Special Wind Regions
description: Reference to geodatabase for special wind regions in contiguous United States

parameters: site-specific values for select special wind regions

title: Regional Climatic Data Usage Outside Hurricane-Prone Regions
description: Conditions for using regional climatic data in lieu of basic wind speeds

data.rule: Regional climatic data shall only be used in lieu of basic wind speeds when specific conditions are met

conditions.value: length of record, sampling error, averaging time, anemometer height, data quality, and terrain exposure

data.then: Permitted to use regional climatic data and reduction in basic wind speed below Figure 26.5-1 is permitted

title: Hurricane-Prone Regions Wind Speed Requirements
description: Restrictions on wind speed determination in hurricane-prone regions

data.rule: In hurricane-prone regions, wind speeds derived from simulation techniques shall only be used with approved procedures, and regional wind-speed data from anemometers is not permitted for hurricane risk areas
data.then: Use only approved simulation and extreme-value statistical analysis procedures
exceptions: Use of regional wind-speed data from anemometers is not permitted for Gulf and Atlantic coasts, Caribbean, or Hawaii

title: Table 26.6-1: Wind Directionality Factor, Kd
description: Directionality factors for various structure types

rows.Structure Type: Buildings - Main wind force resisting system
Chimneys, tanks, and similar structures - Square
Chimneys, tanks, and similar structures - Hexagonal
Chimneys, tanks, and similar structures - Octagonal
Chimneys, tanks, and similar structures - Round
Solid freestanding walls, roof top equipment, and solid freestanding and attached signs
Open signs and single-plane open frames
Trussed towers - Triangular, square, or rectangular
Trussed towers - All other cross sections

metadata.qc_notes: Note: *Directionality factor Kd = 0.95 shall be permitted for round or octagonal structures with nonaxisymmetric structural systems.

title: Surface Roughness B
description: Definition of Surface Roughness Category B

data.definition: Urban and suburban areas, wooded areas, or other terrain with numerous, closely spaced obstructions that have the size of single-family dwellings or larger

title: Surface Roughness C
description: Definition of Surface Roughness Category C

data.definition: Open terrain with scattered obstructions that have heights generally less than 30 ft (9.1 m). This category includes flat, open country and grasslands

title: Surface Roughness D
description: Definition of Surface Roughness Category D

data.definition: Flat, unobstructed areas and water surfaces. This category includes smooth mudflats, salt flats, and unbroken ice

title: Exposure B for Buildings 30 ft or Less
description: Conditions for applying Exposure B to low buildings

data.rule: For buildings or other structures with a mean roof height less than or equal to 30 ft, Exposure B shall apply where Surface Roughness B prevails upwind for specified distance
data.then: Apply Exposure B

title: Exposure B for Buildings Greater than 30 ft
description: Conditions for applying Exposure B to tall buildings

data.rule: For buildings or other structures with a mean roof height greater than 30 ft, Exposure B shall apply where Surface Roughness B prevails upwind for specified distance

conditions.value: greater of 2600 ft or 20 times building height

data.then: Apply Exposure B

title: Equation (26.8-1): Topographic Factor, Kzt
description: Formula for calculating topographic factor to account for wind speed-up effects

K2.source: factor from Figure 26.8-1, not less than 0

K3.source: factor from Figure 26.8-1, not less than 0

title: Table 26.9-1: Ground Elevation Factor, Ke
description: Table providing ground elevation factors to adjust for air density

title: Wind Speed-Up Conditions
description: Conditions that must be met for including topographic effects

data.rule: Wind speed-up effects shall be included when site conditions and locations meet all specified conditions

conditions.parameter: H for Exposures C and D

data.then: Include topographic factor K_zt in wind load calculations

title: Topographic Multipliers
description: Multiplier values K1, K2, and K3 for different topographic features and ratios

title: K2 Factor Calculation
description: Formula for calculating the K2 factor based on distance from crest

K2.source: factor to account for reduction in speed-up with distance upwind or downwind of crest

x.source: distance (upwind or downwind) from the crest to the site of the building or other structure

Lh.source: distance upwind of crest to where the difference in ground elevation is half the height of hill or escarpment

title: Parameters for Speed-Up over Hills and Escarpments
description: Attenuation parameters for different hill shapes and exposure categories

title: Figure 26.8-1: Topographic factor, Kzt
description: Figure showing topographic factor calculation methodology

data.description: Diagram and associated plotting figure used to determine the topographic factor Kzt from hill, ridge, and escarpment geometry and the building position relative to the crest.

title: Equation (26.10-1): Velocity Pressure, qz
description: Formula for calculating velocity pressure at height z in imperial units

Kz.source: velocity pressure exposure coefficient, see Section 26.10.1

title: Equation (26.10-1.SI): Velocity Pressure, qz
description: Formula for calculating velocity pressure at height z in SI units

Kz.source: velocity pressure exposure coefficient, see Section 26.10.1

title: Table 26.10-1: Velocity Pressure Exposure Coefficients, Kh and Kz
description: Tabular values for velocity pressure exposure coefficients by height and exposure category

columns.name: Height above Ground Level, z or h (ft)
Height above Ground Level, z or h (m)

title: Risk Category for Rooftop Equipment Wind Speed
description: Requirements for determining risk category for rooftop structures and equipment

data.rule: The basic wind speed, V, used in determination of design wind loads on rooftop structures, rooftop equipment, and other building appurtenances shall consider the Risk Category equal to the greater of the following
data.then: Use the greater of: 1) Risk category for the building on which the equipment or appurtenance is located, or 2) Risk category for any building or other structure to which the equipment or appurtenance provides a necessary service

title: Gust-Effect Factor for Rigid Structures
description: Simplified gust-effect factor for rigid buildings and structures

data.rule: The gust-effect factor for a rigid building or other structure is permitted to be taken as 0.85
data.then: Use gust-effect factor = 0.85

title: Natural Frequency Requirements for Flexible Buildings
description: Requirements for buildings requiring natural frequency analysis

data.rule: Buildings meeting specified conditions are required to be calculated in accordance with Section 26.11.3
data.then: Calculate natural frequency per Section 26.11.3 for structural steel, concrete, or masonry buildings
data.else: Use properly substantiated analysis to establish fundamental natural frequency
exceptions: Low-rise buildings as defined in Section 26.2 are permitted to be considered rigid

title: Equation (26.11-1): Effective Length, Leff
description: Formula for calculating effective length in the direction under consideration

Leff.source: effective length in the direction under consideration

hi.source: height above grade of level i

Li.source: building length at level i parallel to the wind direction

title: Equation (26.11-2): Approximate Natural Frequency, na
description: Approximate natural frequency for structural steel moment-resisting frame buildings

title: Equation (26.11-2.SI): Approximate Natural Frequency, na
description: Approximate natural frequency for structural steel moment-resisting frame buildings in SI units

title: Equation (26.11-3): Approximate Natural Frequency, na
description: Approximate natural frequency for concrete moment-resisting frame buildings

title: Equation (26.11-3.SI): Approximate Natural Frequency, na
description: Approximate natural frequency for concrete moment-resisting frame buildings in SI units

title: Equation (26.11-4): Approximate Natural Frequency, na
description: Natural frequency calculation for equivalent height buildings

title: Equation (26.11-4.SI): Approximate Natural Frequency, na
description: Natural frequency calculation for equivalent height buildings in SI units

title: Equation (26.11-5): Approximate Natural Frequency, na
description: Alternative natural frequency calculation for shear wall buildings

title: Equation (26.11-5.SI): Approximate Natural Frequency, na
description: Alternative natural frequency calculation for shear wall buildings in SI units

title: Equation (26.11-8): Background Response, Q
description: Background response Q for gust-effect factor calculation

Lz.source: integral length scale of turbulence at equivalent height

title: Equation (26.11-9): Integral Length Scale of Turbulence, Lz
description: Length scale calculation for turbulence at equivalent height

Lz.source: integral length scale of turbulence at equivalent height

title: Equation (26.11-6): Gust-Effect Factor, G
description: Simplified gust-effect factor calculation for rigid buildings and structures

Iz.source: intensity of turbulence at height z

title: Equation (26.11-7): Intensity of Turbulence, Iz
description: Turbulence intensity calculation for gust-effect factor

Iz.source: intensity of turbulence at height z

z.source: equivalent height of building or structure

title: Equation (26.11-10): Gust-Effect Factor, Gf
description: Complete gust-effect factor calculation for flexible buildings and structures

Iz.source: intensity of turbulence at height z

title: Equation (26.11-11): Resonant Peak Factor, gR
description: Peak factor calculation for resonant response

title: Equation (26.11-12): Resonant Response Factor, R
description: Resonant response factor calculation for flexible buildings

R_L.source: function related to integral length scale

title: Table 26.11-1: Terrain Exposure Constants
description: Constants for different terrain exposures used in wind load calculations

title: Gust-effect Factor for Rigid Buildings
description: Permitted methods for calculating gust-effect factor for rigid buildings

data.rule: For rigid buildings or other structures as defined in Section 26.2, the gust-effect factor shall be taken as 0.85 or calculated by formula
data.then: Use G = 0.85 or calculate using Equation 26.11-6

title: Gust-effect Factor for Flexible Buildings
description: Required calculation method for gust-effect factor for flexible buildings

data.rule: For flexible buildings or other structures as defined in Section 26.2, the gust-effect factor shall be calculated by the specified formula
data.then: Calculate using Equation 26.11-10

title: Equation (26.11-16): Mean Hourly Wind Speed, Vz
description: Formula for calculating mean hourly wind speed at equivalent structure height

V_z.source: mean hourly wind speed at height z

title: Equation (26.11-16.SI): Mean Hourly Wind Speed, Vz
description: SI version of formula for calculating mean hourly wind speed

V_z.source: mean hourly wind speed at height z

title: Rational Analysis Alternative
description: Permits alternative rational analysis for gust-effect factor determination

data.rule: Rational analysis from recognized literature is permitted as alternative to specified procedure
data.then: May use any rational analysis defined in recognized literature in lieu of procedures in Sections 26.11.4 and 26.11.5

title: Combined Gust-Effect Factor Limitations
description: Prohibits separate calculation when combined factors are provided

data.rule: Where combined gust-effect factors and pressure coefficients are given in figures and tables, the gust-effect factor shall not be determined separately
data.then: Do not calculate gust-effect factor separately

title: Building Enclosure Classification
description: Required classification of buildings for internal pressure determination

data.rule: Buildings and other structures shall be classified as enclosed, partially enclosed, partially open, or open
data.then: Classify according to definitions in Section 26.2
exceptions: If a building satisfies both 'open' and 'partially enclosed' definitions, classify as 'partially open'

title: Building Opening Assessment
description: Required determination of openings in building envelope

data.rule: A determination shall be made of the amount of openings in the building envelope
data.then: Each building wall shall be assumed as the windward wall for consideration of openings with respect to remaining building envelope

title: Glazed Opening Protection Requirement
description: Protection requirements for glazed openings in hurricane-prone regions

data.rule: Glazed openings in Risk Category II, III, or IV buildings located in hurricane-prone regions shall be protected
data.then: Protect glazed openings as specified in this section

title: Wind-Borne Debris Region Glazing Protection
description: Locations where glazed openings require protection from wind-borne debris

data.rule: Glazed openings shall be protected in specified wind-borne debris regions

value: within 1 mile from mean high water line with Exposure D upwind and basic wind speed >= 130 mi/h

data.then: Protect glazed openings in accordance with Section 26.12.3.2
exceptions: Glazing located more than 60 ft above ground and more than 30 ft above aggregate-surfaced roofs within 1,500 ft of building shall be permitted to be unprotected

title: Glazed Opening Protection Requirements
description: Testing and performance requirements for glazing protection systems

data.rule: Glazing requiring protection shall be impact resistant or protected with impact-protective system
data.then: Systems shall be subjected to missile test and cyclic pressure differential test per ASTM E1996 and E1886
exceptions: Where approved, other testing methods and/or performance criteria may be used to demonstrate compliance

title: ASTM E1996 Impact Test Standard
description: Referenced standard for missile test and cyclic pressure differential test

title: ASTM E1886 Test Standard
description: Referenced standard for compliance testing procedures

title: ANSI/DASMA 115 Door Testing Standard
description: Referenced standard for sectional, rolling, and flexible door glazing tests

title: Internal Pressure Coefficient Determination
description: Method for determining internal pressure coefficients

data.rule: Internal pressure coefficients (GCpi) shall be determined from Table 26.13-1
data.then: Base determination on building enclosure classifications from Section 26.12

title: Table 26.13-1: Main Wind Force Resisting System and Components and Cladding (All Heights): Internal Pressure Coefficient, (GCpi), for Enclosed, Partially Enclosed, Partially Open, and Open Buildings (Walls and Roof)
description: Internal pressure coefficients for different building enclosure classifications

rows.Criteria for Enclosure Classification: Ao is less than the smaller of 0.01Ag or 4 ft² (0.37 m²), and Aoi/Agi ≤ 0.2
Ao > 1.1Aoi, and Ao > the lesser of 0.01Ag or 4 ft² (0.37 m²), and Aoi/Agi ≤ 0.2
A building that does not comply with Enclosed, Partially Enclosed, or Open classifications
Each wall is at least 80% open

title: Equation (26.13-1): Internal Pressure Reduction Factor, Ri
description: Reduction factor for internal pressure coefficient in large unpartitioned volumes

Ri.source: reduction factor for internal pressure coefficient

Aog.source: total area of openings in the building envelope (walls and roof)

title: ANSI A58.1 Minimum Design Loads
description: Reference to ANSI minimum design loads standard

data.target: ANSI A58.1, Minimum Design Loads for Buildings and Other Structures, American National Standards Institute, 1982

title: ANSI/DASMA 115 Testing Standard for Doors
description: Standard method for testing sectional, rolling, and flexible doors

data.target: ANSI/DASMA 115, Standard Method for Testing Sectional Doors, Rolling Doors, and Flexible Doors: Determination of Structural Performance under Missile Impact and Cyclic Wind Pressure, Door and Access Systems Manufacturers Association International, 2017

title: ASTM E330 Structural Performance Test Method
description: Standard test method for structural performance under uniform static air pressure difference

data.target: ASTM E330, Standard Test Method for Structural Performance of Exterior Windows, Doors, Skylights, and Curtain Walls by Uniform Static Air Pressure Difference, ASTM International, 2014

title: ASTM E1886 Impact and Cyclic Pressure Test Method
description: Standard test method for performance under missile impact and cyclic pressure differentials

data.target: ASTM E1886, Standard Test Method for Performance of Exterior Windows, Curtain Walls, Doors, and Impact Protective Systems Impacted by Missile(s) and Exposed to Cyclic Pressure Differentials, ASTM, 2019

title: ASTM E1996 Windborne Debris Performance Specification
description: Standard specification for performance under windborne debris impact in hurricanes

data.target: ASTM E1996, Standard Specification for Performance of Exterior Windows, Curtain Walls, Doors and Impact Protective Systems Impacted by Windborne Debris in Hurricanes, ASTM International, 2020

title: CAN/CSA A123.21 Dynamic Wind Uplift Test Method
description: Standard test method for dynamic wind uplift resistance of membrane-roofing systems

data.target: CAN/CSA A123.21, Standard Test Method for the Dynamic Wind Uplift Resistance of Membrane-Roofing Systems, CSA Group, 2014
